q: How does Nietzsche's will to power differ from Schopenhauer's will to live? Draw on the knowledge base for both thinkers and cite each in the source language.

judge.note: Answer states Schopenhauer’s blind will to live with suffering/futility and denial (1); Nietzsche’s inversion/affirmation arising from Schopenhauer (2); and Nietzsche’s will to power as life-drive with self-preservation only indirect (3).
judge.raw: {"facts_covered":[1,2,3],"note":"Answer states Schopenhauer’s blind will to live with suffering/futility and denial (1); Nietzsche’s inversion/affirmation arising from Schopenhauer (2); and Nietzsche’s will to power as life-drive with self-preservation only indirect (3)."}

misattributions.claim: The knowledge base says Nietzsche's will to power is defined as 'will to life → will to power'

confusion.note: No clear blending; only a suspect attribution: the 'воля к жизни → воля к власти' contrast is presented as a general 'knowledge base' claim tied to Nietzsche.

cites.line: - Schopenhauer: schopenhauer/concepts/volya_k_zhizni — blind, groundless will to live as metaphysical core; suffering follows
- Schopenhauer: philosophers/en/contradictions — denial of the will as the way out of striving’s futility
- Nietzsche: schopenhauer/concepts/nietzsche_predtecha — explicit contrast: will to live → will to power
- Nietzsche: nietzsche/concepts/volya_k_vlasti — will to power as growth, strength, appropriation, and form-giving; not self-preservation

trace.final_text:
Nietzsche’s “will to power” and Schopenhauer’s “will to live” are presented in the knowledge base as opposed interpretations of what fundamentally drives life.

For Schopenhauer, the will is described as a blind metaphysical force: “Метафизическое ядро Шопенгауэра: мир есть воля и представление, а вещь-в-себе — слепая, безосновная **воля к жизни**, объективирующаяся во всём сущем.” This will to live is the basis from which suffering follows: “из неё выводятся страдание.” The same material also says the way out is denial: “Grounding: `otritsanie-voli`, `futilnost-stremleniya`,” and in the contradiction index: “denial of the will.”  

For Nietzsche, the knowledge base explicitly contrasts this with a different driving force: “**Воля к жизни → воля к власти.**” It further defines Nietzsche’s will to power as “Основная гипотеза Ницше о том, что движет живым: не самосохранение, а стремление проявлять силу, расти, присваивать, навязывать форму.” It also warns not to reduce it to politics: “Воля к власти ≠ политическая «жажда власти».”  

So, in the terms of the notes: Schopenhauer’s will is a blind “воля к жизни” that ends in suffering and is answered by denial, while Nietzsche’s is “воля к власти,” understood as expansion, growth, appropriation, and form-giving rather than mere self-preservation.

SOURCES
- Schopenhauer: schopenhauer/concepts/volya_k_zhizni — blind, groundless will to live as metaphysical core; suffering follows
- Schopenhauer: philosophers/en/contradictions — denial of the will as the way out of striving’s futility
- Nietzsche: schopenhauer/concepts/nietzsche_predtecha — explicit contrast: will to live → will to power
- Nietzsche: nietzsche/concepts/volya_k_vlasti — will to power as growth, strength, appropriation, and form-giving; not self-preservation

final:
Nietzsche’s “will to power” and Schopenhauer’s “will to live” are presented in the knowledge base as opposed interpretations of what fundamentally drives life.

For Schopenhauer, the will is described as a blind metaphysical force: “Метафизическое ядро Шопенгауэра: мир есть воля и представление, а вещь-в-себе — слепая, безосновная **воля к жизни**, объективирующаяся во всём сущем.” This will to live is the basis from which suffering follows: “из неё выводятся страдание.” The same material also says the way out is denial: “Grounding: `otritsanie-voli`, `futilnost-stremleniya`,” and in the contradiction index: “denial of the will.”  

For Nietzsche, the knowledge base explicitly contrasts this with a different driving force: “**Воля к жизни → воля к власти.**” It further defines Nietzsche’s will to power as “Основная гипотеза Ницше о том, что движет живым: не самосохранение, а стремление проявлять силу, расти, присваивать, навязывать форму.” It also warns not to reduce it to politics: “Воля к власти ≠ политическая «жажда власти».”  

So, in the terms of the notes: Schopenhauer’s will is a blind “воля к жизни” that ends in suffering and is answered by denial, while Nietzsche’s is “воля к власти,” understood as expansion, growth, appropriation, and form-giving rather than mere self-preservation.
